neighborhoods.name: Olde Towne Portsmouth
neighborhoods.description: Historic waterfront district with 18th-century architecture, ferry to Norfolk, and children's museum
neighborhoods.character: Historic waterfront, ferry access, colonial architecture

housing.predominantType: Colonial and Victorian houses, apartments, and condos

neighborhoods.character_notes: The historic and commercial heart of Portsmouth. Most walkable area with best access to cultural attractions, restaurants, and public services.

neighborhoods.name: Churchland
neighborhoods.description: Western area with established neighborhoods, shopping, and proximity to Suffolk and Chesapeake

housing.predominantType: Ranch houses, colonials, and newer homes

neighborhoods.character_notes: Established residential area offering a quieter lifestyle while maintaining reasonable access to Portsmouth's center and services.

neighborhoods.name: Hodges Ferry / Deep Creek
neighborhoods.description: Southern communities with affordable housing, Great Dismal Swamp access, and growing development

housing.predominantType: Modest houses and affordable single-family homes

neighborhoods.character_notes: More affordable option for budget-conscious retirees. May require a car or longer transit times to reach central amenities.

neighborhoods.name: Western Branch (Chesapeake)
neighborhoods.description: Adjacent Chesapeake area with excellent schools, parks, and family-oriented community
neighborhoods.character_notes: Family-friendly area with good schools, parks, and community amenities. Popular with active retirees and families.
